q: The term radioactivity is also known as nuclear _______.

Which rays have the greatest ionizing power? _______ rays.

A _______ counter is used to detect nuclear radiation.

Isotopes are atoms of the same element with different numbers of _______.

Energy of the sun and stars is produced by nuclear _______.

Gamma radiation is emitted when a nucleus is in an _______ state.
The symbol for an alpha particle is _______.

In a cloud chamber, _______ particles produce thin and curly tracks.
The mass of an alpha particle is about _______ times the mass of a proton.
In _______ decay, a neutron changes into a proton and an electron.
Nuclear radiation can cause genetic _______.
One _______ (Bq) is the unit of activity.
High energy _______ radiation is emitted during gamma decay.

A _______ source is used in thickness monitoring systems.

A _______ radiation is not deflected by magnetic fields.
